records.rationale: This property is consumed by deacon's feature installer at install time, but the facet here is a feature-authoring schema constraint (how an author must declare it in devcontainer-feature.json). Deacon (consumer-only, constitution II) never validates feature files against this schema; it reads the value through its own lenient FeatureMetadata model and has no reference-aligned conformance coverage for the authoring rule. Out of consumer-only scope.

Feature-authoring schema constraint: it governs the structure/metadata an author must place in devcontainer-feature.json. Feature authoring and feature-file validation are permanently out of deacon's consumer-only scope (constitution II); deacon only consumes already-published features and never validates them against this schema.

Annotation-kind facet (title/description/examples/$schema) carrying no externally observable, testable behavior. The devContainerFeature schema is a feature-authoring surface deacon (consumer-only, constitution II) never validates (research Decision 11, rule 1).

Annotation-kind facet (title/description/examples/$schema) carrying no externally observable, testable behavior. The devContainerFeature schema is a feature-authoring surface deacon (consumer-only, constitution II) never validates (research Decision 11, rule 1).

Out of consumer-only scope.
records.notes: deacon's FeatureMetadata::validate() enforces a non-empty feature id at consume time (integration_features::test_parse_invalid_fixture), but this is deacon's own consumer check, not validation of the feature file against this authoring schema.

records.rationale: Feature-authoring schema constraint: it governs the structure/metadata an author must place in devcontainer-feature.json. Feature authoring and feature-file validation are permanently out of deacon's consumer-only scope (constitution II); deacon only consumes already-published features and never validates them against this schema.
records.notes: deacon models a feature's version as optional (Option<String>) and does NOT enforce this authoring `required: version` rule — a deliberate lenient-consumer divergence, and feature-file validation is out of scope (constitution II).

Deacon (consumer-only, constitution II) never validates feature files against this schema; it reads the value through its own lenient FeatureMetadata model and has no reference-aligned conformance coverage for the authoring rule. Out of consumer-only scope.
records.notes: deacon reads the option type/enum/default to validate CONSUMER-supplied option values (integration_features::test_option_value_validation_with_fixtures); the authoring-shape constraint on the declaration itself is out of consumer scope.

Feature-authoring schema constraint: it governs the structure/metadata an author must place in devcontainer-feature.json. Feature authoring and feature-file validation are permanently out of deacon's consumer-only scope (constitution II); deacon only consumes already-published features and never validates them against this schema.
